title: IPv4 Addressing, CIDR, VLSM, and Subnet Design · Independent Network Trial
summary: Create an address plan for departments, infrastructure, guests, point-to-point links, growth, and route summarization. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits.
Identify RFC1918, APIPA, loopback, unspecified, limited broadcast, multicast, and public addressing behavior.
Calculate network address, broadcast address, usable host range, prefix length, and address count for CIDR blocks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.
